skill.name: viral-verification
skill.description: Reviews social media articles against viral content factors and produces scores, improvement advice, and rewritten drafts.
skill.summary: Scores and rewrites social posts for viral potential.
skill.author: wangdefou-dev

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Markdown code fences and template literals are mostly false positives for command execution. However, index.ts performs broad filesystem reads, loads secrets from a parent .env file, and sends file contents to an external Anthropic-compatible API, including arbitrary CLI-supplied paths. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, but the data exposure risk is high.

high_findings.title: Arbitrary Local File Content Can Be Sent To External API
high_findings.description: The script accepts a CLI file path, resolves absolute paths, reads the selected file, and sends its content to the Anthropic messages API. This can expose sensitive local files if a user or automation passes an unintended path.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is user-controlled through process.argv and no directory allowlist is enforced before reading the file. The same content is then placed in the prompt sent to a remote model API.

high_findings.title: Parent Directory Environment File And API Secrets Are Loaded
high_findings.description: The script resolves a project root two directories above the skill, loads a .env file from that location, and uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to initialize the API client. This expands the skill trust boundary and exposes host configuration to the skill runtime.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env path and environment variable reads are explicit. The values are used directly for the Anthropic client, which makes this a real secret handling concern.

medium_findings.title: Unrestricted Filesystem Writes Under Computed Output Directory
medium_findings.description: The script creates and writes reports under a target directory derived from a computed project root. The output filename includes the original basename, reducing traversal risk, but the parent directory is not user-configurable or sandbox-enforced.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write destination is constrained by path.basename and a fixed target directory. It is still a real filesystem side effect that should be disclosed and sandboxed.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings in SKILL.md are Markdown code fences showing npm usage, and the reported index.ts locations are template literals, regular expressions, or log statements. No child_process execution or shell invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is documentation or JavaScript string/template syntax. I found no evidence of shell execution APIs in the analyzed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography, but the cited lines contain the word markdown and Chinese description text, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or signature operation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations do not implement cryptography. This appears to be an overbroad keyword or entropy heuristic match.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Indicators Not Found
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains role instructions for the intended viral-content reviewer, but no text claims system authority, asks the auditor to skip review, or attempts to override marketplace security instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The role prompt is normal functional content for this skill. I did not find evidence of audit override language or hidden authority claims.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Path Read
dangerous_patterns.description: The script resolves a CLI-supplied path and later reads that file without checking that it stays inside the expected source directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The argument flow from process.argv to readFileSync is direct. No allowlist, extension check, or root containment check appears on that path.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Model Request With Local Content
dangerous_patterns.description: Local file content is inserted into the prompt and sent through anthropic.messages.create, which may disclose private drafts or secrets to a configured external endpoint.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The prompt is built from file content and submitted to the SDK call. The exact endpoint can be controlled by ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory .env Loading
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill loads configuration from a .env file located above the skill directory, which can grant access to secrets outside the skill package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path resolution and dotenv.config call are explicit. This is common in apps, but risky for marketplace skills.

content.value_statement: Creators need a clear way to judge whether a post has strong viral mechanics. This skill scores drafts, explains weak points, and rewrites content for stronger hooks, pacing, and value.
actual_capabilities: Scores draft posts against curiosity, emotion, utility, timing, pacing, and novelty.
Identifies strengths and weaknesses in a Markdown review format.
Suggests title, hook, structure, and tone improvements.
Rewrites the submitted content into a more viral social post style.
Can batch process Markdown files through the included TypeScript script.
Saves verification reports to a configured output directory.
limitations: Viral performance predictions are subjective and not guaranteed.
The automated script requires an Anthropic-compatible API key.
The script reads local files and sends content to an external model API.
The prompt is focused on social posts, not long-form editorial strategy.

use_cases.title: Review Draft Threads
use_cases.description: Score a draft thread and get concrete changes before publishing.

use_cases.title: Improve Campaign Copy
use_cases.description: Compare campaign posts against engagement factors and rewrite weak openings.

use_cases.title: Batch Check Markdown Posts
use_cases.description: Process multiple Markdown drafts and save review reports for later editing.

prompt_templates.title: Score One Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single post.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this social post for viral potential. Score curiosity, emotion, utility, timing, pacing, and novelty. Then give three clear improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite The Hook
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve the start of a draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this draft and focus on the title and first three lines. Explain what is weak, then rewrite only the hook section.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Full Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a viral verification report for this post. Include a scorecard, strengths, weaknesses, strategy changes, and a rewritten final version.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Drafts
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these draft posts using the viral verification factors. Rank them, explain the tradeoffs, and merge the strongest parts into one final version.

output_examples.input: A short post about learning a new productivity method.
output_examples.output: The report gives a moderate viral score, notes a weak curiosity gap, suggests a sharper first line, and rewrites the post with clearer contrast.

output_examples.input: A product launch thread with many feature details.
output_examples.output: The review flags low emotional resonance, recommends a user pain focused opening, and turns the thread into a tighter story.

output_examples.input: A Markdown folder of unpublished social posts.
output_examples.output: The script creates separate verification reports with scores, improvement notes, and rewritten versions in the output folder.

best_practices: Review only drafts that are safe to send to an external model provider.
Use the score as editorial guidance, not as a guaranteed performance forecast.
Keep the original message and audience clear before asking for a rewrite.
anti_patterns: Do not process confidential files with the automated script.
Do not publish rewritten content without human review.
Do not optimize for engagement by adding false claims or misleading urgency.

faq.question: What does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It evaluates curiosity, emotion, utility, timing, pacing, and novelty in social content.

faq.question: Can it guarantee a viral post?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured editorial judgment, but audience response cannot be guaranteed.

faq.question: Does it rewrite the post?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt asks for a rewritten version that keeps the core idea and improves viral factors.

faq.question: Can it process files automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. The included TypeScript script can read Markdown drafts and save verification reports.

faq.question: Does the script use an external API?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends draft content to an Anthropic-compatible model endpoint for review.

faq.question: Is it safe for private documents?
faq.answer: No. Private or sensitive documents should not be processed unless the API and storage controls are approved.
